title: Donkey Domestication and Genetic History
passage:
For thousands of years, donkeys have been critical for propelling human civilizations forward. They have helped pull wheeled vehicles, carry travelers and move goods across the world. But where and when these animals first became intertwined with humans has been a mystery. Now, researchers have used the genomes of over 200 donkeys to trace their domestication back to a single event around 7,000 years ago in East Africa, about 3,000 years before humans tamed horses. The team published their findings in the journal Science this month.

"Through their DNA, the animals are telling their history themselves," co-author Samantha Brooks, an equine researcher at the University of Florida, says in a statement. "We usually only get the human's side of history through written accounts, but of course written history does not always record exactly how something happened. Looking at these DNA sequences, we get a biological testimony to the environment these animals lived in and the experiences they survived."

The researchers examined 207 genomes from modern donkeys living in 31 countries across the globe. They also looked at genomes from 15 wild equids and 31 earlier donkeys that lived between about 4,000 and 100 years ago. The team reconstructed the animals' evolutionary tree and used computer models to pinpoint the domestication event, when herders in Kenya and the Horn of Africa tamed wild asses. They then traced how the animals spread across the rest of the continent and into Europe and Asia about 2,500 years later.

Though it is still unclear why the original domestication happened, Science News' Freda Kreier reports that the event coincided with the Sahara growing larger and drier. "Donkeys are champions when it comes to carrying stuff and are good at going through deserts," co-author Ludovic Orlando, an evolutionary biologist at Paul Sabatier University in France, tells the publication. Prehistoric humans may have enlisted donkeys' help in navigating the expanding Sahara.

Researchers say these findings could help put donkeys in the spotlight. The animals could benefit from more research: currently, there are no published genomes from donkeys located south of the equator in Africa. But understanding where the animals were first domesticated could guide archaeologists to a narrow region to search for insights about the original tamed donkeys. Not only does understanding the equines' genetic makeup help reveal their contribution to human history, but it also might improve their management in the future, as climate change alters the planet's environment, write the authors.

questions.questionText: What can be learned about donkeys from paragraph 1?

options.A: They seemed mysterious to human ancestors.
options.C: They were tamed at an earlier time than horses.
options.D: They were vividly portrayed by ancient travelers.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 1 says where and when donkeys first became intertwined with humans has been a mystery.
questions.location: Paragraph 1: where and when these animals first became intertwined with humans has been a mystery.
questions.thinkingMethod: 定位 paragraph 1，抓住 mystery 这个核心信息。

questions.questionText: What message is conveyed in Brooks' statement?

options.A: The earliest habitats of donkeys are hardly traceable.
options.B: It is increasingly easy to read donkeys' DNA sequences.
options.C: Written accounts contain vital clues for donkey research.
options.D: Genetic analysis offers insight into the history of donkeys.

questions.explanation: Brooks stresses that DNA lets the animals tell their own history.
questions.location: Paragraph 2: Through their DNA, the animals are telling their history themselves.
questions.thinkingMethod: 引号题要概括引语主旨，DNA/history 对应 genetic analysis。

questions.questionText: In their study, the researchers investigated how donkeys ______.

options.B: survived with the help of herders.

questions.explanation: The researchers traced how donkeys spread across Africa and into Europe and Asia.
questions.location: Paragraph 3: traced how the animals spread across the rest of the continent and into Europe and Asia.
questions.thinkingMethod: spread 与 dispersed 是同义替换。

questions.questionText: As to why the original domestication of donkeys happened, Orlando ______.

options.C: calls for evidence from the Sahara.
options.D: holds a different view from Kreier.

questions.explanation: Orlando suggests prehistoric humans may have used donkeys to navigate the expanding Sahara.
questions.location: Paragraph 4: Prehistoric humans may have enlisted donkeys' help in navigating the expanding Sahara.
questions.thinkingMethod: may have 表明是可能解释，不是确定结论。

questions.questionText: The authors think that their research could help with ______.
questions.explanation: The final paragraph says the research might improve donkey management in the future.
questions.location: Last paragraph: it also might improve their management in the future.
questions.thinkingMethod: 目的题直接找 could help / might improve。

vocabulary.sentence: For thousands of years, donkeys have been critical for propelling human civilizations forward.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 数千年来，驴一直是推动人类文明前进的关键力量。
vocabulary.note: critical for/to 表示“对……至关重要”。

vocabulary.sentence: For thousands of years, donkeys have been critical for propelling human civilizations forward.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 数千年来，驴一直是推动人类文明前进的关键力量。
vocabulary.note: 常用于抽象意义：推动发展、进步。

vocabulary.sentence: For thousands of years, donkeys have been critical for propelling human civilizations forward.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 数千年来，驴一直是推动人类文明前进的关键力量。
vocabulary.note: 历史文化类文章高频词。

vocabulary.sentence: But where and when these animals first became intertwined with humans has been a mystery.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 但这些动物最早在何时何地与人类紧密联系在一起，一直是个谜。
vocabulary.note: intertwined with 表示“与……交织或关联”。

vocabulary.sentence: But where and when these animals first became intertwined with humans has been a mystery.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 但这些动物最早在何时何地与人类紧密联系在一起，一直是个谜。
vocabulary.note: 本篇第21题定位词。

vocabulary.sentence: Now, researchers have used the genomes of over 200 donkeys to trace their domestication back to a single event around 7,000 years ago in East Africa.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 如今，研究人员利用200多头驴的基因组，将其驯化追溯到约7000年前东非的一次单一事件。
vocabulary.note: 科学研究类文章常见词。

vocabulary.sentence: Now, researchers have used the genomes of over 200 donkeys to trace their domestication back to a single event around 7,000 years ago in East Africa.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 如今，研究人员利用200多头驴的基因组，将其驯化追溯到约7000年前东非的一次单一事件。
vocabulary.note: trace...back to... 表示“把……追溯到……”。

vocabulary.sentence: Now, researchers have used the genomes of over 200 donkeys to trace their domestication back to a single event around 7,000 years ago in East Africa.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 如今，研究人员利用200多头驴的基因组，将其驯化追溯到约7000年前东非的一次单一事件。
vocabulary.note: domestic 是“家养的；国内的”。

vocabulary.sentence: Now, researchers have used the genomes of over 200 donkeys to trace their domestication back to a single event around 7,000 years ago in East Africa.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 如今，研究人员利用200多头驴的基因组，将其驯化追溯到约7000年前东非的一次单一事件。
vocabulary.note: 与 multiple 形成常见反义干扰。

vocabulary.sentence: The finding dates to about 3,000 years before humans tamed horses.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 这一发现的时间比人类驯服马匹早约3000年。
vocabulary.note: tamed horses 是第21题干扰信息来源。

vocabulary.sentence: Through their DNA, the animals are telling their history themselves, co-author Samantha Brooks says in a statement.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 合著者 Samantha Brooks 在声明中说，通过它们的DNA，这些动物正在自己讲述自己的历史。
vocabulary.note: 新闻和研究报道中常见。

vocabulary.sentence: Co-author Samantha Brooks, an equine researcher at the University of Florida, says in a statement.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 合著者、佛罗里达大学马科动物研究员 Samantha Brooks 在声明中说。
vocabulary.note: 本文中包括驴等马科动物。

vocabulary.sentence: Co-author Samantha Brooks, an equine researcher at the University of Florida, says in a statement.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 合著者、佛罗里达大学马科动物研究员 Samantha Brooks 在声明中说。
vocabulary.note: 题干 Brooks' statement 直接对应。

vocabulary.sentence: We usually only get the human's side of history through written accounts.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 我们通常只能通过文字记载了解人类一方的历史。
vocabulary.note: written accounts 表示“文字记录”。

vocabulary.sentence: Looking at these DNA sequences, we get a biological testimony to the environment these animals lived in.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 通过研究这些DNA序列，我们获得了关于这些动物生活环境的生物学证据。
vocabulary.note: DNA sequence 是固定搭配。

vocabulary.sentence: Looking at these DNA sequences, we get a biological testimony to the environment these animals lived in and the experiences they survived.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 通过研究这些DNA序列，我们获得了关于这些动物生活环境和生存经历的生物学证据。
vocabulary.note: 这里不是法庭证词，而是“证据、证明”。

vocabulary.sentence: The researchers examined 207 genomes from modern donkeys living in 31 countries across the globe.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 研究人员检查了来自全球31个国家的207个现代驴基因组。
vocabulary.note: 研究类文章中常表示“研究、考察”。

vocabulary.sentence: The researchers examined 207 genomes from modern donkeys living in 31 countries across the globe.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 研究人员检查了来自全球31个国家的207个现代驴基因组。
vocabulary.note: across the globe = across the world。

vocabulary.sentence: They also looked at genomes from 15 wild equids and 31 earlier donkeys.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 他们还研究了15个野生马科动物和31头更早期驴的基因组。
vocabulary.note: 比 equine 更偏学术。

vocabulary.sentence: The team reconstructed the animals' evolutionary tree and used computer models to pinpoint the domestication event.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 研究团队重建了这些动物的进化树，并使用计算机模型精确确定驯化事件。
vocabulary.note: re- 前缀表示“再次、重新”。

vocabulary.sentence: The team reconstructed the animals' evolutionary tree and used computer models to pinpoint the domestication event.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 研究团队重建了这些动物的进化树，并使用计算机模型精确确定驯化事件。
vocabulary.note: evolution 的形容词。

vocabulary.sentence: The team reconstructed the animals' evolutionary tree and used computer models to pinpoint the domestication event.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 研究团队重建了这些动物的进化树，并使用计算机模型精确确定驯化事件。
vocabulary.note: 常表示准确定位原因、时间或位置。

vocabulary.sentence: They then traced how the animals spread across the rest of the continent and into Europe and Asia about 2,500 years later.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 随后他们追踪了这些动物约2500年后如何扩散到非洲大陆其他地区并进入欧洲和亚洲。
vocabulary.note: 第23题 dispersed 的同义替换。

vocabulary.sentence: They then traced how the animals spread across the rest of the continent and into Europe and Asia about 2,500 years later.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 随后他们追踪了这些动物约2500年后如何扩散到非洲大陆其他地区并进入欧洲和亚洲。
vocabulary.note: 地理类文章高频词。

vocabulary.sentence: Though it is still unclear why the original domestication happened, the event coincided with the Sahara growing larger and drier.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 尽管最初的驯化为何发生仍不清楚，但这一事件与撒哈拉沙漠变得更大、更干旱同时发生。
vocabulary.note: 第24题直接出现。

vocabulary.sentence: Science News' Freda Kreier reports that the event coincided with the Sahara growing larger and drier.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 《科学新闻》的Freda Kreier报道称，这一事件与撒哈拉沙漠变得更大、更干旱同时发生。
vocabulary.note: coincide with 是常考搭配。

vocabulary.sentence: Prehistoric humans may have enlisted donkeys' help in navigating the expanding Sahara.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 史前人类可能曾借助驴的帮助穿越不断扩张的撒哈拉沙漠。
vocabulary.note: 既可指实际导航，也可指处理复杂处境。

vocabulary.meaning: 聚光灯；关注中心；突出显示
vocabulary.sentence: Researchers say these findings could help put donkeys in the spotlight.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 研究人员表示，这些发现可能有助于让驴受到更多关注。
vocabulary.note: put...in the spotlight = 让……成为关注焦点。

vocabulary.sentence: Understanding where the animals were first domesticated could guide archaeologists to a narrow region to search for insights.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 了解这些动物最初在哪里被驯化，可以引导考古学家到更窄的区域寻找线索。
vocabulary.note: archaeology 是“考古学”。

vocabulary.sentence: It also might improve their management in the future, as climate change alters the planet's environment.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 随着气候变化改变地球环境，这也可能改善未来对它们的管理。
vocabulary.note: 第25题 better management 的原文对应。

longSentences.sentence: Not only does understanding the equines' genetic makeup help reveal their contribution to human history, but it also might improve their management in the future, as climate change alters the planet's environment, write the authors.
longSentences.analysis: Not only...but also... 连接两个方向：揭示历史贡献；改善未来管理。as 引导背景状语。

articleStructure: 提出驴的驯化谜题；介绍 DNA 研究如何提供证据；说明研究过程与扩散路径；推测驯化原因；总结研究价值。
notes: PDF 文字层乱码，内容通过 PDF 页面截图核对，并整理为网站可读 JSON。
